metadata.name: Maintain Synchronized Time
metadata.description: Change the time zone on a server and configure NTP time synchronization with chronyd
objectives: Change the time zone on a server
Configure the server to synchronize its time with an NTP time source
prerequisites: Basic understanding of system time and time zones
Familiarity with timedatectl and chronyc commands

tasks.name: Verify time zone set to America/Port-au-Prince
tasks.description: The servera time zone should be configured for Haiti
tasks.success_message: Time zone correctly set to America/Port-au-Prince
tasks.failure_message: Time zone not set to America/Port-au-Prince
hints: Find time zones with: tzselect or timedatectl list-timezones

tasks.name: Verify chrony configured with classroom NTP server
tasks.description: The /etc/chrony.conf should reference classroom.example.com as NTP source
tasks.success_message: chrony configured with classroom.example.com NTP server
tasks.failure_message: chrony.conf missing classroom.example.com NTP server configuration
hints: The iburst option speeds up initial synchronization

tasks.name: Verify NTP synchronization is enabled
tasks.description: Time synchronization should be enabled via timedatectl

tasks.name: Verify chronyd service is running
tasks.description: The chronyd service should be active and running
hints: Start service: sudo systemctl start chronyd

tasks.name: Verify system clock is synchronized
tasks.description: The system clock should be synchronized with the NTP server
hints: Wait a few seconds after enabling NTP for synchronization
Verify: timedatectl (check System clock synchronized: yes)

tasks.name: Verify classroom.example.com is active NTP source
tasks.description: The chronyc sources should show classroom.example.com as the active time source
tasks.success_message: classroom.example.com is the active NTP source
tasks.failure_message: classroom.example.com is not the active NTP source
hints: Look for ^* prefix indicating the active source
If not synchronized, restart chronyd: sudo systemctl restart chronyd
